title: Geum octopetalum
intro: Geum octopetalum, also known as the Canadian avens, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The roots and rhizomes of this plant have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Geum octopetalum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Geum octopetalum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make teas, tinctures, and salves for topical applications.

This family is a large and diverse group of plants that include roses, apples, pears, plums, cherries, apricots, peaches, plums, and many others.
